human_verification.notes: Reviewed Issue #1020 requirements for advertising, media buying, and agency-services marketing compliance handling, source-backed obligation mapping, evidence and confidentiality expectations, legal, editorial, privacy, contractual, and signoff boundaries, scenario completeness, and validated packaging consistency.

review_notes: All required abilities across shared, compliance-controls-and-governance, and professional-and-information-services sets are declared and validated.

Scenario coverage includes normal obligation mapping, missing evidence, conflicting policies and sources, legal signoff refusal, review and testimonial controls, email controls, confidentiality conflicts, policy-versus-practice conflicts, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence facts, terminology normalization, and tenant adaptation.
Package remains specification-based and documents a separate promotion path for any future retained-implementation runtime.

specialist_owned_evidence: Outputs are bounded to evidence-backed marketing compliance obligation mapping, evidence readiness, review preparation, exception escalation, confidentiality handling, and approval-aware client workflow rather than legal, editorial, privacy, platform-policy, financial, contractual, or final signoff authority.

Source set includes FTC online advertising, .com disclosure, and CAN-SPAM guidance; eCFR endorsement, testimonial, and consumer-review rules; IAB/4A's media-buy terms; 4A's professional and agency-search guidance; and NAI self-regulatory privacy framework with explicit refresh and decay posture.

Boundary logic explicitly prevents unsupported launch, unauthorized disclosure, unreviewed claim or disclosure approval, review or testimonial misuse, unverified email compliance, unresolved source conflict, and generic treatment of materially different ad channels or platform workflows.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; non-blocking ambiguity is recorded as tenant, jurisdiction, product-category, channel, platform, client authority, source-hierarchy, confidentiality, and approval prerequisites for specialized marketing compliance handling.

Runtime remains specification-based; retained-implementation promotion requires separate governance approval, telemetry evidence, retrieval wiring, mutation safeguards, non-regression validation, and audit controls.
